MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-09-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant, Lauren Lipton
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Tom Paoli, Esqq. (415.999.8863; tompaoli9@gmail.com) for Plaintiff.
Laila Rahimi-Keshari, Esq. (415.276.0702; Laila.Rahimi-Keshari@libertymutual.com) of Law Offices of Santana & Vierra for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LAUREN LIPTON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied.

The key facts are few and undisputed. Defendant and plaintiff are yoga enthusiasts. While they were posing at a studio, defendant's foot contacted plaintiff's hand, injuring it. (PSO pp. 7, 12.)

Defendant's motion has one ground: she owed "no duty" to plaintiff because the "primary assumption of risk doctrine" applies. (Not. 2:1-2; Memo. 5:1-3.) Under that doctrine, a person has no duty to protect others from risks of "inherently dangerous" recreational activities. (Kahn v. East Side Union High School Dist. (2003) 31 Cal.4th 990, 1003; see also Knight v. Jewett (1992) 3 Cal.4th 296, 314-15.) This is an exception to the "general rule" that "persons have a duty to use due care to avoid injury to others." (Id.)
Defendant, who has the initial burden of proof, cites no case that has ever held yoga to be an inherently dangerous activity. (CCP 473c(p).) No doubt this is because yoga lacks the attributes of activities subject to the primary assumption of risk doctrine. In yoga, there is nothing akin to another person's "carelessly thrown baseball" or "a carelessly extended elbow" in basketball. (Knight, 3 Cal.4th at 316.) Rather, injuries caused by other persons are rare in yoga. (Wadhawan Dec. 3:3-9; Calazans Dec. 1:16-22.)

As defendant acknowledges: "The determination of what constitutes an 'inherent risk' is a legal question for the court." (Memo. 6:9.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
